item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am utterly charmed by this. His description was totally accurate, and I dig that his natural energy drinks are compliant with the laws of thermodynamics. Go science!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…subject begins accelerating rapidly upwards, reaching a velocity of 11.186 km/s within 30 seconds. Subject will experience forces in excess of 50 G during the course of this acceleration.

Shouldn't that be a comma, not a period?

Edit: sorry, missread as "km/h".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a high-quality scip, I trust dado. +1

  • Multizig never mentions anything about "rad"; I'd suggest adding that in there so that it comes from something multizig says instead of dado just coming up with it on their own
  • If the webcrawlers flag anything with any mention of "dado juice", then there's no need to have them also search for "dado juice energy drink" (since the second phrase contains the first). A better way to do it would be to flag pages that contain both "dado" and either "juice" or "energy drink" (and make sure that 'dado' is really referring to the person, since it means 'dice' in Spanish)
  • I don't think that "Amazon prime pages" are a thing, just "Amazon pages", but that might be worth keeping in there for the dado reference

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A cognitohazard is defined as something that is triggered when perceived by any of the senses. In this case, it is activated by the sense of taste, hence why it is also tagged "gustatory".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I only have one question: Why is this tagged as "cognitohazard"?
A really good file, though. Gotta love dado.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know why i love the dado skips that much but i do. Upvoted. The "i have amazon prime" line just. I dunno. Makes me click upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"dado airlines giv trabel to u verry cheap"
dado
Really, having a pill that makes you "fly" seems like something dado would do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
much rad and burning

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Enh, this reads as yet another "Dr. Nick by way of Cobrastan" bit, and the kicker reminds me of some of the more formulaic entries in the Wanderers' Library (Mundane thing + unexpected user/subject = hilarity).

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So I ran http://dadolaundryandtan.net through google and I found a travel agency thing. Does this mean we also have dado airlines?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The first guys (including multizig and MaliceAF) who tried it and turned into the human torch / the real-world equivalent of the Hulk / got blasted into space didn't have the luxury of seeing the Amazon listing though. One of them just asked dado to make them some stuff, gave him their address, and it turned up in the mail a day or two later.

And in fairness, they did ask for something rad, something like speed… an upper that would give them lots of energy and let them burn down the town and send their "party" into space… and dado delivered, to the letter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An IRC channel… on an http webpage? Was that deliberate?

Also, should the 1-star-reviewing tentacle monster possibly saying "not recommended for Euclidian organisms", rather than "non-Euclidian"? The latter would probably deal with the stuff better, and it's probably in that category itself…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the twist you threw on there, though I agree that it's more of the one-note dado joke. But it's well written and funny so fuck it, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this so much, the contrast between the clinical tone and dado's laughable English makes this very enjoyable, +1.

Las Vegas Amazon Fuilfillment Center

This may be a typo, by the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
dado seems like a cool dude

Apparently no one reads the fine print. Not for human consumption smh.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Containment procedures didn't really say how much you need to consume to get effects or whether it could work partially, but if testing is suspended (for good reason) then the Foundation probably wouldn't figure out the threshold and details.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked the article but I think it is redundant to search "dado juice" and "dado juice energy drink". The only juice one would be enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In the section where dado explains that he is not for parties, but make medicine for hare grow and also make people old and horny, might I suggest cross-links?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: no don't you'll end up on neptune like me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just read that one, didn't know about it. That was a good read, we should start tagging these dado articles with a dado tag for quick navigation, yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought I already did that. Guess not. Problem solved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Um… Does this have a name yet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the customers for the hare growth pill seemed pretty happy, im guessing they followed instructions after realizing they were holding literal magic that they didnt know jack shit about lmao

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The chat log actually reminds me a lot of dado's first article, so I really dig this. This is a lot more than him just misunderstanding a request; he's actively trying to get stuff done in his own way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved the IRC bit. I almost thought having one of those drinks was a good idea…
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And to think you were already getting tired of dado!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
THIS. You got it.

I wish I could upvote this comment. I wanted to add a new wrinkle to the dado character/mythos- that not only does he employ the anomalous in his product, but he ACTIVELY MARKETS to the anomlaous as well, possibly explaining further why his dealings with humans are so disasterous in nature.

But don't tell the others. They think it's a mindless, witless, formulaic copy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is my second Dado article, I've read. I do like that he's just now branching into other markets, and that the drinks are literally three forms of energy. Energy drinks gone wrong. I think to offset this impression of Dado making only bad products, someone should write a few with his products doing what was needed. That or Dado is just really good at self-marketing, hence why he keeps getting business. I enjoyed the premise and execution of this SCP +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This honestly actually brings me up to an upvote for this article. I somehow completely glossed over this entirely.

Well played, DrConnors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: seconding this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
dado to me is like a sit-com character at this point. He has some kind of new funny shenanigan with each new episode, just with the later seasons the jokes start getting stale.

I like the anomaly itself, and the images made me laugh, but the whole *whoa dado types in a funny way and gives the client something he misinterpreted!* gag isn't really doing it for me anymore. I just wish we didn't have dado articles coming out every other day, this would have been an upvote otherwise, keeping it as a no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dado is starting to feel played out by now and formulaic. Some jokes here and there are funny (the "no red cows no wings" got me) but I feel dado is being less mysterious with all the articles about him. In his first three articles, he was a shady drug dealer and now he is doing all sorts of stuff from energy drinks to tan and laundry. I mean, this article implies he does business with eldritch horrors which could mean he is one too.

Also, due to the stuff that goes wrong with dado stuff, why is that the guys friend would recommend dado? I mean, wouldn't everyone by now, in regard to the other articles, be aware to never bother with dado for the horrific shit that happens when using his stuff?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, there are some who consider downvoting without reading an article to be malicious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ehhh. Isn't quite doing it for me. It feels like a generic sort of dado thing, and generic dado is starting to wear off for me. And the maliceAF bit seems sort of wedged in, but whatever.1 Novote.

Footnotes
1. Nothing personal, malice :V

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was sacrificed to the altar of dado, may his glory be praised; poor maliceaf will be salty that I beat him to it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm on the fence. It and Dado are competently written but it all feels rather derivative. I enjoyed the twist that Dado fuckery now extends beyond our three-dimensions, but as the latest installment in the International Dado Multimedia Franchise, it didn't tickle any of my particular funny-bones. Glue-tan was pretty good, but it feels like I've read this before. That's the biggest thing, and it makes me want to novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, I was about to downvote because I couldn't see even dado making something so obviously, unmistakably harmful, in spite of how funny it was. However, the reveal at the end that the product was never intended for humans to drink brought the portrayal of dado back into believability for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will give this article this; dado writing "sry caps key broke" with magic marker on an aluminum canister is maybe one of the funniest things I've read in quite a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yessss… let the hate flow through you…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a simple man I see Dado I downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought this was going to be the tipping point for dado, that it'd be awful and no one would write any more.

It wasn't. I laughed. Interview log had a few high-pitched chuckles throughout.

I have a feeling the next one will be the tipping point, but… I said that last time

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Was starting to get tired of dado, but hey, you have progressed his narrative. And also written something that's funny as shit.

I mean, fuck's sake, dado, your caps lock key is broken? You wrote that shit with a magic marker, bro. Goddamn, that and the product description was worth an upvote alone.

<dado>: i likes parties too but not wanting to poop in your party

The peoples, they go to the parties and they poop?

EDIT: Though I forgot to add, the one honestly weird thing was seeing actual site usernames in the chat log. <.< Like, that was just strange. And this is dado we're talking about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's fun, but now I'm wondering if we've gone too far with the dado skips. I'll keep this a no-vote for now; even though the article's good, it's nothing we haven't seen already in the dado canon (is the dado canon a thing? we should make it a thing), and the last review feels out of place. I think ending it on the amazon page would be sufficient.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This thing is hilarious, and I'm not even mad it isn't a Joke SCP.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've seen a lot of dado recently, and ideas seem to be running low. However, the effort put into the images pushes this to a +1 from me. Also liked the inclusion of other SCPers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: is this just another dado story except it’s an energy drink instead of a pill.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My biggest question is… how do people keep finding out about Dado, and why do they still ask him to make stuff?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm generally unenthused about dado articles at this point, and the core misunderstanding is a bit too straightforward for me to appreciate it from a comedic standpoint. I don't think there's really anything that can be done about that now, but I'm at -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I can fix that. Lemme just load up dadoshop cc…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted! It's a fun article. I do wonder if it's not missing a little something (and I can't really put my finger on what—something to do with the effects, maybe?), but I really appreciate the work you put into the images. I also liked the containment procedures quite a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 4.21 mb image is probably excessive even for dado dado is use photoediting software and can make fine size image for your foam loaf time, yes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoting alone for the work you put into the images. Hats off, mate

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to djkaktus for being a mensch, and to stormbreath for the crit!

Image rights: Mine and free for anyone to do whatever. Picture is of aluminum laboratory specimen containers, written on in low-effort style with a wet-erase sharpie.

<DrConnors>: I need a volunteer
<DrConnors>: I need one SCPper who is willing to be a victim of a horrible fate
<DrConnors>: Just your screenname, really, in a skip I'm writing
<MaliceAF>: wait, an in-universe character?
<Bennings>: The most satisfying parts of worldbuilding are when you solve two problems at once
<multizig>: DrConnors: I volunteer as tribute
<MaliceAF>: DrConnors: I voluntee- DAMMIT
<DrConnors>: THIS CONTACT IS SEALED
<MaliceAF>: ;-;
MaliceAF slinks back into the shadows
<DrConnors>: I might be able to squeeze two of ya'll in there
<MaliceAF>: on the other hand, I do have a habit of killing off my own characters before anyone else can
<DrConnors>: Just sign in blood on the dotted line, please
<MaliceAF>: whose blood?
<DrConnors>: Doesn't matter
MaliceAF raises knife, giggles quietly
<MaliceAF>: hehehehehe
<multizig>: O-O
<multizig>: noplsibegyounottodothis
MaliceAF slashes wildly, misses everyone, cuts his cheek, falls face-first onto the contract and accidentally signs it
<MaliceAF>: whoopsie
<DrConnors>: Away to my writing hole.

Edit: let the hatred for dado flow through you
